title: Cinnamomum filipes
intro: Cinnamomum filipes, also known as Indonesian cinnamon or Weh cinnamon, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Lauraceae, native to the Mentawai Islands of Indonesia. The plant is valued for its aromatic bark, which contains bioactive constituents such as cinnamaldehyde, linalool, and camphene, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents in Cinnamomum filipes have been shown to exhibit potential therapeutic benefits, including antibacterial and antifungal activities, and may also contribute to its use as a traditional remedy for respiratory issues and menstrual disorders. In traditional medicine, Cinnamomum filipes is commonly prepared as a tea, oil, or powder, with the most common herbal preparations including cinnamon tea, cinnamon oil, and cinnamon capsules or tablets.
